# `dev_signing_key.pem` and `dev_signing_key_pub.hex`

These are committed test fixtures, NOT a real signing key.

`dev_signing_key.pem` is an unencrypted ECDSA P-256 private key used
by the **dev server** (`app/server.py`) and the **e2e test suite** to
sign the bundle manifest on the fly. This lets dev e2e tests
exercise the same signature-verify path that the production service
worker runs, without needing the maintainer to type a passphrase on
every `pytest` invocation.

`dev_signing_key_pub.hex` is the matching public key in
raw-uncompressed-point hex (130 chars). It's also baked into
`app/static/sw.js`'s `DEV_PUBLIC_KEY_HEX` constant so the service
worker can verify dev signatures.

## Why is committing a private key OK here?

Because the service worker's `selectPublicKeyHex()` accepts the dev
public key **only when `self.location.origin` is `http://localhost`
or `http://127.0.0.1`**. The production origin
(`https://fellows.globaldonut.com`) refuses to trust the dev key —
no matter who signs with it, the bundle won't verify on prod.

So anyone with a copy of this repo can run the app locally without a
passphrase prompt, but no one can use this key to push poisoned
updates to real users.

## The real prod signing key

Generated by `python scripts/keygen_signing_key.py` and lives in
`~/.fellows/signing-key.enc.pem` on the maintainer's laptop (mode
0600, encrypted with a passphrase). The matching public key is in
`PROD_PUBLIC_KEY_HEX` in `app/static/sw.js`.

See `docs/DevOps.md` § Signing keys and bundle verification for the
full operator runbook.
